title: Attijariwafa Bank Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Attijariwafa Bank Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Attijariwafa Bank Egypt?
faq.answer: Attijariwafa Bank Egypt is a commercial bank operating in Egypt and a subsidiary of Attijariwafa Bank Group, one of Africa's leading banking and financial institutions. The bank was formerly known as Barclays Bank Egypt and operates as a universal bank offering both retail and corporate banking services. With a network of branches across key Egyptian cities and over 120,000 retail customers, Attijariwafa Bank Egypt serves individuals, small and medium-sized enterprises, and major corporations throughout the country.

faq.question: When was Attijariwafa Bank Egypt founded?
faq.answer: The bank has roots dating back to 1864 when Barclays Bank began operations in Egypt. On May 3, 2017, Barclays Bank PLC transferred its entire shareholding in Barclays Bank Egypt S.A.E. to Attijariwafa Bank Group, with the parent company acquiring 100% of the former Barclays subsidiary. On November 3, 2017, the bank was officially registered as Attijariwafa Bank Egypt S.A.E. and began operating under the Attijariwafa brand and logo. This acquisition was part of Attijariwafa Bank Group's strategic expansion across Africa.

faq.question: Where is Attijariwafa Bank Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: Attijariwafa Bank Egypt is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt. The bank maintains its primary operations and executive leadership in the capital city. The bank's customer service hotline can be reached at +202 2461 5000, providing support to customers across the country. The headquarters coordinates the bank's operations across its 56-65 branches distributed throughout 18 Egyptian cities.

faq.question: Where does Attijariwafa Bank Egypt operate?
faq.answer: Attijariwafa Bank Egypt operates throughout Egypt with a network of 56-65 branches located in 18 major cities, including Cairo, Nasr City, and other key urban centers. The bank also maintains an extensive network of over 83 ATMs, providing customers with convenient access to banking services across the country. The branch network covers major commercial and residential areas, ensuring accessibility for both retail and corporate customers throughout Egypt.

faq.question: What services does Attijariwafa Bank Egypt offer?
faq.answer: Attijariwafa Bank Egypt offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services. For retail customers, the bank provides savings accounts (including the Wafa Premium monthly savings account and Wafa Baseet accounts), personal loans, and various payment cards including Prestige Debit Cards, Platinum Credit Cards, and Cashback Cards. The bank also offers the WafaPoints loyalty program, a travel rewards program for cardholders. Corporate banking services include treasury operations, foreign currency exchange, international money transfers through MoneyGram, trade finance, and cash management solutions. The bank serves a substantial corporate client base including leading international, regional, and domestic companies. Digital banking services are delivered through the bank's mobile app, which allows customers to manage accounts, track transactions, and access banking services anytime and anywhere. The app features biometric security and real-time notifications. The bank also provides internet banking services with online registration for convenient digital banking access.

faq.question: What makes Attijariwafa Bank Egypt unique?
faq.answer: Attijariwafa Bank Egypt benefits from its connection to the Attijariwafa Bank Group, which operates in 25-26 countries across Africa, Europe, and the Middle East. This international network provides the bank with significant resources and expertise to deliver sophisticated banking solutions to the Egyptian market. The acquisition of the former Barclays operations gave Attijariwafa Bank Egypt an established reputation and existing customer relationships dating back over 150 years. The bank's highly professional and skilled employees serve a diverse client base, combining the legacy of Barclays' presence in Egypt with the pan-African reach and expertise of the Attijariwafa Group. As of end-2023, Egypt represented approximately 4.7% of the Attijariwafa Bank Group's total assets, making it one of the group's significant markets outside Morocco. The bank's position within this major African financial group enables it to facilitate trade and investment between Egypt and other African markets.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Attijariwafa Bank Egypt?
faq.answer: Attijariwafa Bank Egypt is a universal commercial bank operating in Egypt. It provides services across multiple banking segments including retail banking, small and medium enterprise (SME) banking, and corporate banking. The bank is regulated by the Central Bank of Egypt and operates under Egyptian banking regulations. As a universal bank, it offers a complete range of deposit-taking, lending, payment services, treasury operations, and trade finance services to serve the comprehensive financial needs of individuals and businesses in the Egyptian market.
